title: Thelypteris ligulata
taxonomy: Thelypteris ligulata is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris and is a member of the order Polypodiales. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants. This classification indicates the fern's evolutionary relationships with other plants. Thelypteris ligulata is a distinct species within the fern family.

Its medicinal uses include treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. intro: Thelypteris ligulata, also known as the Ligule Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions, characterized by its delicate fronds and unique ligule structure. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
